summary: Excerpt from Dr. Thomas Teskey's 'Breaking Traditions' arguing that steel horseshoes damage horses and urging farriers and vets to promote barefoot trimming and hoof boots.
Carole Herder summarizes seven ways to read a horse's health from hoof condition, covering sole, frog, bars and heel/toe length.
Carole Herder essay on patience in horsemanship, comparing metal shoes to short-term fixes and advocating taking time with your horse.
Final excerpt from Dr. Thomas Teskey's 'Breaking Traditions', a veterinary plea against prescribing horseshoes in favor of proper trimming, movement and diet.
Carole Herder profiles sabre fencer and horseman Russ Krachun and his step-by-step partnership program at the Mane Event Trainers Challenge.
Carole Herder profiles Tom Durocher of Fishing Lake Metis Settlement, Canada's only Monty Roberts instructor, and his horsemanship journey.
Carole Herder makes the cost case for going barefoot and using Cavallo hoof boots versus repeated farrier shoeing bills, citing boot prices from $119-$144.
Customer testimonial from a professional barrel racer and farrier praising Cavallo hoof boots for barefoot trail and gravel-ground use.
Carole Herder explains equine Ting points, acupressure points on the coronary band tied to organ meridians, for diagnosis and natural healing.
Carole Herder reflective essay questioning whether riders compete for themselves or their horses, and recognizing signs of equine pain.
Carole Herder explains how red light (LED) therapy benefits horses by penetrating skin to reduce pain, inflammation and aid cellular healing.
Carole Herder lists ten ways to bond with a horse you can't ride, including grooming, massage, lunging, long-lining and agility courses.
